title: Work-order schema is a v1 contract whose engine implements a demo subset — four required fields never consumed, and the catalog schema asserts a scope clamp that does not run
description: The work-orders schema requires input_contract, context_blocks, output_contract, and scope; the loop originally consumed none of their semantics. Two are now honored (fixed): the dispatch input is validated against input_contract via the canonical pi-context AJV validator before compile/spawn (validateWorkOrderInput), and the capability grant is clamped to wo.scope.operations (clampToScope intersects the composed grant, so a caller grant of [write,bash] under scope.operations [write] dispatches with --tools write). Two remain unconsumed: context_blocks is never injected into the dispatched agent, and output_contract is never validated against the agent's returned output — both have zero references in pi-agent-dispatch. The catalog schema's scope description historically asserted a clamp that did not run; the clamp now runs, so that assertion is satisfied for scope, while the schema still declares context_blocks/output_contract semantics the engine does not implement. Class: schema-contract ahead of engine (sibling: ContextBlockRef, pi-jit-agents types.ts — documents its deferral). Direction settled by git dates: the schema landed one day ahead of the loop's demo-subset engine.

evidence.reference: dispatchTargetAgent: input validated against wo.input_contract (validateWorkOrderInput) before compile/spawn; the composed grant clamped to wo.scope.operations (clampToScope) becomes the subprocess --tools allowlist — both FIXED. context_blocks and output_contract still have zero consumption in this file.
required nine fields; still declares context_blocks + output_contract semantics the engine does not implement (scope clamp now satisfied)
Break 3: per-field verification with quotes, git-date archaeology, the WO-001 scope demonstration

impact: Filed work-orders carry dead contract fields authors reasonably rely on: inputs are never validated, context never injected, outputs never checked, and a narrower wo.scope.operations does not narrow the grant. Any substrate installing the catalog schema inherits contract text promising enforcement that does not exist.
proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014, the two remaining fields: inject wo.context_blocks into the dispatched pi subprocess (resolve the referenced blocks and pass them into the agent's context across the process boundary) and validate the agent's returned output against wo.output_contract post-return (the subprocess must emit structured output the loop can check) — or amend the schema to stop declaring semantics the engine will not implement, dual-surface, with per-field regression pins. The scope.operations clamp and input_contract validation are already implemented with unit pins (clampToScope, validateWorkOrderInput).
